statement.focus_question: Given $n$ distinct points $A\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ must there be a point $x\in A$ such that\[\#\{ d(x,y) : y \in A\} \gg n^{1-o(1)}?
statement.normalized: Given $n$ distinct points $A\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ must there be a point $x\in A$ such that\[\#\{ d(x,y) : y \in A\} \gg n^{1-o(1)}?\]Or even $\gg n/\sqrt{\log n}$?
statement.original: Given $n$ distinct points $A\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ must there be a point $x\in A$ such that\[\#\{ d(x,y) : y \in A\} \gg n^{1-o(1)}?\]Or even $\gg n/\sqrt{\log n}$?
